content.problemStatement:
Suppose you have an array B (0‚â§Bi‚â§109)B \ (0 \leq B_i \leq 10^9)B (0‚â§Bi‚Äã‚â§109) of length MMM, and a positive integer K (1‚â§K<M)K \ (1 \leq K \lt M)K (1‚â§K<M).

You are allowed to do the following operation on BBB:


Select xxx indices i1,i2,‚Ä¶,ix (1‚â§ij‚â§M)i_1, i_2, \ldots, i_x \ (1 \leq i_j \leq M)i1‚Äã,i2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,ix‚Äã (1‚â§ij‚Äã‚â§M), such that (ij+1‚àíij)‚â•K(i_{j + 1} - i_j) \geq K(ij+1‚Äã‚àíij‚Äã)‚â•K for all 1‚â§j<x1 \leq j \lt x1‚â§j<x;
Subtract 111 to BijB_{i_j}Bij‚Äã‚Äã for all 1‚â§j‚â§x1 \leq j \leq x1‚â§j‚â§x.
For the array BBB and integer KKK, let `score`(B,K)(B, K)(B,K) denote the **minimum** number of operations required to make all the elements of BBB equal to 000.

You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN non-negative integers, and a positive integer PPP.

Find the value ‚àëi=1N‚àëj=iNF(i,j)\sum_{i=1}^{N} \sum_{j=i}^{N} F(i,j)‚àëi=1N‚Äã‚àëj=iN‚ÄãF(i,j), where F(i,j)=F(i,j)=F(i,j)= `score`(A[i,j],P)(A[i,j],P)(A[i,j],P).

Since the sum might be too large, please print it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

Note that A[i,j]A[i, j]A[i,j] denotes the subarray [Ai,Ai+1,‚Ä¶,Aj][A_i, A_{i+1}, \ldots, A_j][Ai‚Äã,Ai+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Aj‚Äã].


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains two positive integers NNN and PPP ‚àí-‚àí the length of AAA and the parameter integer PPP.
The second line contains NNN space separated integers, A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã ‚àí-‚àí denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, print on a new line, the required sum modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Here A=[1,2,3]A = [1, 2, 3]A=[1,2,3] and P=1P = 1P=1:

score(A[1,1],1)=1(A[1,1],1) = 1(A[1,1],1)=1 since we choose the index 111 and subtract 111 from A1A_1A1‚Äã. Similarly score(A[2,2],1)=2(A[2,2],1) = 2(A[2,2],1)=2 and score(A[3,3],1)=3(A[3,3],1) = 3(A[3,3],1)=3.
score(A[1,2],1)=2(A[1,2],1) = 2(A[1,2],1)=2:

For the first operation, we choose 222 indices i1=1i_1 = 1i1‚Äã=1 and i2=2i_2 = 2i2‚Äã=2 and subtract 111 from both elements. The array becomes [0,1][0, 1][0,1].
For the second operation, we choose 111 index i1=2i_1 = 2i1‚Äã=2 and subtract 111 from Ai1A_{i_1}Ai1‚Äã‚Äã. The array becomes [0,0][0, 0][0,0].


score(A[2,3],1)=3(A[2,3],1) = 3(A[2,3],1)=3
score(A[1,2,3],1)=3(A[1,2,3],1) = 3(A[1,2,3],1)=3

Thus, sum of scores 1+2+3+2+3+3=141+2+3+2+3+3 = 141+2+3+2+3+3=14.
Test case 222: For all subarrays, we need 000 operations since all elements are already 000.
Thus, sum of scores is 000.
